NN.Runtime.External.Process

  • Lean

Imported by
    Runtime.External.Process.artifactWorkDir
    Runtime.External.Process.resolveCmdFromEnv
    Runtime.External.Process.isCmdAvailable

    Runtime.External.Process.ensureCmdAvailable
    Runtime.External.Process.runStdoutChecked
    Runtime.External.Process.runJsonStdoutChecked

    External process helpers #

    TorchLean frequently calls out to external tools (Python, Julia, ...), typically in the “untrusted producer, trusted checker” pattern:

    • the external tool produces an artifact (often JSON),
    • Lean parses it and checks it against a small trusted kernel.

    This file centralizes utilities shared across wrappers:

    • environment-variable overrides for selecting the executable, and
    • availability checks with consistent optional-dependency error messages, and
    • “run and parse stdout as JSON” helpers with good error messages.

    It does not provide any claims of correctness about the external tool. It only keeps subprocess handling consistent and non-duplicated.

    Artifact paths #

    source
    🔸 coverage
    def Runtime.External.Process.artifactWorkDir (stem : String) :
    System.FilePath

    Build-directory scratch space for subprocess adapters and generated check artifacts.

    Equations
    • Runtime.External.Process.artifactWorkDir stem = { toString := toString ".lake/build/torchlean_" ++ toString stem }
    Instances For

      Executable resolution #

      source
      🔸 coverage
      def Runtime.External.Process.resolveCmdFromEnv (envVar defaultCmd : String) :
      IO String

      Resolve an executable command name, allowing an environment-variable override.

      Example:

      • resolveCmdFromEnv "TORCHLEAN_JULIA" "julia" uses $TORCHLEAN_JULIA if set, otherwise "julia".
      Equations
      • Runtime.External.Process.resolveCmdFromEnv envVar defaultCmd = do let __do_lift ← liftM (IO.getEnv envVar) pure (__do_lift.getD defaultCmd)
      Instances For

        Availability checks #

        source
        🔸 coverage
        def Runtime.External.Process.isCmdAvailable (cmd : String) (args : Array String := #["--version"]) :
        IO Bool

        Check whether a command is available by running it with version-style arguments.

        Any exception (including “executable not found”) is treated as false. This helper is for optional dependencies; callers that require the command should use ensureCmdAvailable so users get a helpful message.

        Equations
        • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
        Instances For
          source
          🔸 coverage
          def Runtime.External.Process.pythonCanImport (modules : Array String) (pythonCmd : String := "python3") :
          IO Bool

          Check whether a Python command can import the requested modules.

          Equations
          • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
          Instances For
            source
            🔸 coverage
            def Runtime.External.Process.ensureCmdAvailable (toolName cmd : String) (args : Array String := #["--version"]) (envVar : Option String := none) :
            IO String

            Require a command to be available and return the command name/path.

            The toolName and envVar fields are only used in error messages. Wrappers such as Julia or Python oracles use this to keep optional-dependency diagnostics consistent across the codebase.

            Equations
            • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
            Instances For

              Running a subprocess and parsing JSON #

              source
              🔸 coverage
              def Runtime.External.Process.runStdoutChecked (ctx cmd : String) (args : Array String) (cwd : Option String := some ".") :
              IO String

              Run a subprocess and return its captured stdout.

              On nonzero exit code, raises IO.userError including stderr. Any exception thrown by the process runner (e.g. executable not found) is propagated to the caller.

              Equations
              • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
              Instances For
                source
                🔸 coverage
                def Runtime.External.Process.runJsonStdoutChecked (ctx cmd : String) (args : Array String) (cwd : Option String := some ".") :
                IO Lean.Json

                Run a subprocess, treat its stdout as a single JSON payload, and parse it.

                On nonzero exit code, raises IO.userError. If JSON parsing fails, raises IO.userError including the raw stdout (which is usually the most helpful debug output).

                Equations
                • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                Instances For
